---
title: "AgeYouth_Rank order Stability including Sequential and Non-Sequential"
author: "Prerana"
date: "2025-10-21"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r Load packages}
library(readxl)             # Loads package for reading Excel files
library(metafor)            # Loads metafor package for conducting meta-analyses
library(psych)              # Loads psych package for descriptive statistics and psychometrics
library(ggplot2)            # Loads ggplot2 for creating visualizations
library(patchwork)          # Loads patchwork to combine multiple ggplots into one
library(dplyr)
library(writexl)

theme_update(              # Updates global ggplot2 theme settings
  plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),            # Center-aligns plot titles
  axis.text.x = element_text(color="black"),        # Sets x-axis text color to black
  axis.text.y = element_text(color="black")         # Sets y-axis text color to black
)

```


```{r Data transformation}
data_youth <- read_xlsx("output/final_combined_highlighted.xlsx")  # Loads the dataset from Excel

head(data_youth)
```

```{r}
# =============================================================
# Add demographic and study columns from long_format_with_newcol
# to final_combined_highlighted based on (1_ID, 1.1_CaseID)
# =============================================================

# --- File paths ---
file_final <- "output/final_combined_highlighted.xlsx"
file_long  <- "output/long_format_with_newcol.xlsx"

# --- Read both files ---
final_df <- read_xlsx(file_final)
long_df  <- read_xlsx(file_long)

# --- Columns to transfer ---
cols_to_add <- c("1_ID", "1.1_CaseID",
                 "11_Mage", "16_CNT", "17_Eth", "18_Female",
                 "Negative/Positive+LS", "30_Covid",
                 "31_WBm", "33_Relt1", "34_Relt")

# --- Check column existence ---
missing_cols <- setdiff(cols_to_add, names(long_df))
if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop("Missing columns in long_format_with_newcol.xlsx: ",
       paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
}

# --- Select and remove duplicates ---
long_keep <- long_df %>%
  select(all_of(cols_to_add)) %>%
  distinct(`1_ID`, `1.1_CaseID`, .keep_all = TRUE)

# --- Merge by IDs ---
data_youth <- final_df %>%
  left_join(long_keep, by = c("1_ID", "1.1_CaseID"))

# --- Optional: save output (uncomment if needed)
 write_xlsx(data_youth, "output/final_combined_output.xlsx")

# --- Confirmation ---
cat("Data merged successfully and stored in object 'data_youth'\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(data_youth), " | Columns:", ncol(data_youth), "\n")

```

```{r convert data type into numeric format}
data_youth$`1.1_CaseID` <- as.numeric(data_youth$`1.1_CaseID`)            # Converts article ID to factor (categorical)
data_youth$`2_Author` <- as.factor(data_youth$`2_Author`)          # Converts author name to factor
data_youth$`11_Mage` <- as.numeric(data_youth$`11_Mage`)
data_youth$`16_CNT` <- as.factor(data_youth$`16_CNT`)
data_youth$`17_Eth` <- as.factor(data_youth$`17_Eth`)
data_youth$`18_Female` <- as.numeric(data_youth$`18_Female`)
data_youth$`Negative/Positive+LS` <- as.numeric(data_youth$`Negative/Positive+LS`)
data_youth$`33_Relt1` <- as.numeric(data_youth$`33_Relt1`)
data_youth$`34_Relt` <- as.numeric(data_youth$`34_Relt`)

```


```{r Relaability Data cleaning and adding avg column}

# Clean column: 33_Relt1
clean_relt1 <- data_youth %>%
  filter(!is.na(`33_Relt1`) & `33_Relt1` != 9999)

mean_relt1 <- round(mean(clean_relt1$`33_Relt1`, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
cat("Mean of 33_Relt1 (rounded to 2 decimals):", mean_relt1, "\n")

data_youth <- data_youth %>%
  mutate(`33_Relt1` = ifelse(is.na(`33_Relt1`) | `33_Relt1` == 9999, mean_relt1, `33_Relt1`))

# Clean column: 34_Relt
clean_relt <- data_youth %>%
  filter(!is.na(`34_Relt`) & `34_Relt` != 9999)

mean_relt <- round(mean(clean_relt$`34_Relt`, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
cat("Mean of 34_Relt (rounded to 2 decimals):", mean_relt, "\n")

data_youth <- data_youth %>%
  mutate(`34_Relt` = ifelse(is.na(`34_Relt`) | `34_Relt` == 9999, mean_relt, `34_Relt`))

# Optional: Check summaries
summary(data_youth$`33_Relt1`)
summary(data_youth$`34_Relt`)


# Calculating Avg for each ID and adding in new column
# Step 1: Calculate row-wise average of 33_Relt1 and 34_Relt
data_youth <- data_youth %>%
  mutate(avg_rel = rowMeans(select(., `33_Relt1`, `34_Relt`), na.rm = TRUE))

# Step 2: Replace NA or NaN in avg_rel with overall mean (rounded to 2 digits)
mean_avg_rel <- round(mean(data_youth$avg_rel, na.rm = TRUE), 2)

data_youth <- data_youth %>%
  mutate(avg_rel = ifelse(is.na(avg_rel) | is.nan(avg_rel), mean_avg_rel, avg_rel))

# Step 3: Round final values and relocate
data_youth <- data_youth %>%
  mutate(avg_rel = round(avg_rel, 2)) %>%
  relocate(avg_rel, .after = `34_Relt`)

```

```{r}
# =============================================================
# Add age-related columns from processed_data_aw.xlsx to data_youth
# =============================================================

# --- File path for adult well-being processed data ---
file_aw <- "output/processed_data_aw.xlsx"

# --- Read the data file ---
data_aw <- read_xlsx(file_aw)

# --- Columns to add ---
cols_to_add_aw <- c("1_ID", "1.1_CaseID",
                    "agem1ic1", "agem1ic2", "agem1ic3",
                    "timelagc", "agem2i")

# --- Check for missing columns ---
missing_cols_aw <- setdiff(cols_to_add_aw, names(data_aw))
if (length(missing_cols_aw) > 0) {
  stop("Missing columns in processed_data_aw.xlsx: ",
       paste(missing_cols_aw, collapse = ", "))
}

# --- Select unique pairs of IDs with required columns ---
aw_keep <- data_aw %>%
  select(all_of(cols_to_add_aw)) %>%
  distinct(`1_ID`, `1.1_CaseID`, .keep_all = TRUE)

# --- Merge with existing data_youth ---
data_youth <- data_youth %>%
  left_join(aw_keep, by = c("1_ID", "1.1_CaseID"))

# --- Optional: save updated dataset
# write_xlsx(data_youth, "output/final_combined_output_with_age.xlsx")

# --- Confirmation ---
cat("Age-related columns added successfully to 'data_youth'\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(data_youth), " | Columns:", ncol(data_youth), "\n")

```


```{r Adding unique ID for each row and count of CaseID}

# Step 1: Add RowID and CaseID_Count
data_youth <- data_youth %>%
  mutate(RowID = row_number()) %>%
  group_by(`1.1_CaseID`) %>%
  mutate(CaseID_Count = n()) %>%
  ungroup()

# Step 2: Reorder columns to place RowID and CaseID_Count after X1.1_CaseID
data_youth <- data_youth %>%
  select(
    '1_ID', '1.1_CaseID', RowID, CaseID_Count, everything()
  )


head(data_youth)

```

# Rank order Stability for Seq and non-Seq
```{r Computation of Effect Sizes of Rank-Order Stability}

# Adjust correlations for reliability (disattenuated correlation) # we decided to skip rank order stability for now
data_youth$corrdis <- with(data_youth, data_youth$WBcorrij / data_youth$avg_rel) #rel?
data_youth$corrdis <- ifelse(data_youth$corrdis >= 0.99, 0.99, data_youth$corrdis)  # Cap extreme values

# Fisher z transformation
data_youth$corrdis_z <- fisherz(data_youth$corrdis)  # or use metafor::fisherz()

# Variance of z (The -3 comes from the mathematical derivation of the sampling distribution of the Fisher z-transformed correlation. When you compute the standard error of the transformed z-score, it accounts for:

#The fact that correlations have a non-normal distribution (especially when 
#r is large or small)
#The Fisher z-transformation stabilizes the variance
#The degrees of freedom adjustment happens as a result of how the variance of z is estimated from the correlation sampling distribution
#It corrects for the bias in small samples.
#It ensures the variance estimate is valid under the Fisher transformation.)

data_youth$v_corr <- 1 / (data_youth$`10_Size` - 3)
data_youth$v_corrdis <- data_youth$v_corr / (data_youth$avg_rel*data_youth$avg_rel)

# Compute 95% Confidence Intervals in z-space
data_youth$ci_lower_z <- data_youth$corrdis - 1.96 * data_youth$v_corrdis
data_youth$ci_upper_z <- data_youth$corrdis + 1.96 * data_youth$v_corrdis

# Back-transform to r (correlation space)
data_youth$ci_lower_r <- fisherz2r(data_youth$ci_lower_z)
data_youth$ci_upper_r <- fisherz2r(data_youth$ci_upper_z)

```



```{r Rank Order Stability: model for Positive affect}
#For Positive affects
dat.positive <- subset(data_youth, subset = data_youth$`Negative/Positive+LS` == 0)
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive)
print(res, digits=3)

fisherz2r(0.789)
lower_bound <- fisherz2r(0.789-1.96 *0.015)
upper_bound <- fisherz2r(0.789+1.96 *0.015)

cat("Confidence interval: lower =", lower_bound, "\n")
cat("Confidence interval: upper =", upper_bound, "\n")

```
```{r for fisherz2r}
library(psych)  # for fisherz2r

# Values
val1   <- fisherz2r(0.6578422)
val_lo <- fisherz2r(0.6408413 )  # lower
val_hi <- fisherz2r(0.6741981 )  # upper

# Print them nicely
cat("Main value :", val1, "\n")
cat("Lower bound:", val_lo, "\n")
cat("Upper bound:", val_hi, "\n")

```

```{r Rank Order Stability: model for Negative affect}
#For negative affects
dat.negative <- subset(data_youth, subset = data_youth$`Negative/Positive+LS` == 1)
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative)
print(res, digits=3)

fisherz2r(0.768 )
lower_bound <- fisherz2r(0.768  - 1.96 *0.036) #lower bound
upper_bound <- fisherz2r(0.768  + 1.96 *0.036) # upper_bound

cat("Confidence interval: lower =", lower_bound, "\n")
cat("Confidence interval: upper =", upper_bound, "\n")
```
```{r for fisherz2r}
library(psych)  # for fisherz2r

# Values
val1   <- fisherz2r(0.645765)
val_lo <- fisherz2r(0.6027403)  # lower
val_hi <- fisherz2r(0.6850456)  # upper

# Print them nicely
cat("Main value :", val1, "\n")
cat("Lower bound:", val_lo, "\n")
cat("Upper bound:", val_hi, "\n")

```

```{r Effect Size Analyses}

#For Positive affects
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive)
print(res, digits=3)

sum(dat.positive.corr$X10_Size)

```


```{r}

# For Negative affects
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative)
print(res, digits=3)

sum(dat.negative.corr$`10_Size`)
```



```{r Age Effects}
# for Positive

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, agem1ic3))
print(res, digits=4)
```

```{r Age Effects}
# For Negative
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(agem1ic1))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, agem1ic3))
print(res, digits=4)

```

```{r Moderator Analyses: 1}
# For positive and Negative Female + Effect size
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(`18_Female`, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(`18_Female`, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

```


```{r Moderator Analyses: 2}
#Female+timelag+age
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = `18_Female`)
print(res, digits=4)

# For P and N, Female + mean age
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(`18_Female`, agem1ic1, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(`18_Female`, agem1ic1, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)
```


```{r Moderator Analyses: 3}
# For P and N, Female + ethnicity
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(`18_Female`, `17_Eth`, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(`18_Female`, `17_Eth`, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)
```


```{r Effect Size Analyses Controlled for Time Lag}
#For Positive
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(timelagc))
print(res, digits=3)


#For Negative
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(timelagc))
print(res, digits=3)

sum(dat.negative.corr$`10_Size`)


```


```{r Age Effects Controlled for Time Lag}

#cor.test(data_youth$WBmi, data_youth$WBtitj)

# For Positive
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.positive, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, agem1ic3, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

# For Negative
res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)

res <- rma.mv(yi=corrdis, V=v_corrdis, random = ~ 1 | `1.1_CaseID`/RowID, data=dat.negative, mods = cbind(agem1ic1, agem1ic2, agem1ic3, timelagc))
print(res, digits=4)
```
